trade.summary: China is South Africa's largest trading partner. South Africa exports minerals (iron ore, platinum, coal, manganese), agricultural products, and chemicals. China exports electronics, machinery, vehicles, and consumer goods. South Africa runs a trade deficit. Mineral exports are critical for Chinese industrial supply chains.
disputes: South African concerns about Chinese competition in manufacturing
Flooding of South African market with cheap Chinese consumer goods
Labor practices at Chinese-owned South African mines

diplomatic.summary: South Africa and China maintain a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership within BRICS. South Africa is a key African pillar of China's Global South engagement. China supported South Africa's post-apartheid development. Both coordinate positions in BRICS, G20, and UN. South Africa has been criticized by the West for hosting Chinese warship exercises and declining to condemn Russia. The 2023 BRICS expansion was held in South Africa.
recent_incidents: South Africa hosted BRICS summit (2023) which expanded membership
South Africa conducted joint military exercises with Russia and China (February 2023)
South Africa refused to arrest Putin if he attended BRICS summit (ICC warrant)
South Africa filed ICJ case against Israel (2024) aligned with China's position

military.summary: Growing military cooperation. Joint naval exercises (IBSAMAR includes China elements, plus bilateral exercises). Chinese arms sales to South Africa. Defense cooperation agreements. Some concern in the West about level of military integration.
incidents: Joint China-Russia-South Africa naval exercises near Cape Town (February 2023)
South African defense procurement from China ongoing

financial.summary: Significant Chinese investment in South African mining, infrastructure, and manufacturing. China's New Development Bank (BRICS bank) finances South African projects. Chinese companies are major investors in the South African mining sector. Growing trade finance ties.

energy.summary: South Africa exports coal to China and is exploring LNG trade. China is interested in South Africa's nuclear energy expansion. Chinese companies have bid for South African renewable energy projects. Cooperation on South Africa's energy transition.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation under BRICS science framework. Collaboration in astronomy (MeerKAT-SKA), mining technology, agriculture, and traditional medicine. Chinese scholarships for South African students. Joint publications increasing.
